text:
Once upon a time, in a little town filled with chirping birds and flickering flames, there lived a wise old owl named Owlinites.

Every morning, Owlinites would glide silently through the treetops, listening to the songs of the dawn. He loved those songs. They made him think of everything lovely.

One particular dawn, Owlinites heard the most beautiful song coming from a Willow Warbler. It was nothing like anything else. Just delightful little peeps.

Owlinites loved warblers. So he flew closer to listen.

It was a song about springtime joy. Warblers did that. They loved spring. They sang about finding butterflies.

Owlinites liked butterflies. They were graceful little things.

He stayed there, listening, feeling better every time they sang.

Afterward, they just danced.

Oh, how Owlinites enjoyed watching.

He taught them dance moves. They were such good dancers.

After summer, they slowed down. But
